hostBios.host-1: Local guide in Mexico City with 5 years of experience. I love showing the secrets of my city.
hostBios.host-2: Professional photographer. I capture your best moments at the most iconic spots in Barcelona.
hostBios.host-3: Japanese-Spanish translator. I help you communicate in Tokyo without any hassle.
hostBios.host-4: Expert in Bogota nightlife. VIP reservations at the best venues.
hostBios.host-5: Sommelier and lover of Parisian gastronomy. I'll get you the best table with a view.
hostBios.host-map-1: I was born in San Rafael and know every corner of Atuel Canyon. Certified sommelier — I combine outdoor adventure with the best wine tastings in the region.
hostBios.host-map-2: Third-generation gaucho. My horseback rides through the San Rafael hills show you the Mendoza only locals know.
hostBios.host-map-3: Professional chef specialized in Mendoza cuisine. I organize traditional asado with vineyard views and gastronomic tours through the best wineries and restaurants in San Rafael.
hostBios.host-map-4: Bilingual guide and licensed driver. I handle communication and transportation so you can enjoy San Rafael worry-free.
hostBios.host-map-5: Professional landscape and portrait photographer. I capture unique moments among vineyards, mountains and the colors of Atuel Canyon.
hostBios.host-map-6: Certified rafting and water sports instructor. 8 years guiding adventures on the Atuel River. Safety and adrenaline guaranteed.
hostBios.host-map-7: Cook and amateur astronomer. By day I teach you the secrets of Mendoza cuisine, by night I guide you under Argentina's clearest starry sky.
hostBios.host-map-8: Cycling and extreme adventure enthusiast. I organize bike routes through wineries and zip-lines at Atuel Canyon for thrill-seekers.

services.srv-1: Secret tour of the Historic Center
services.srv-5: Table with a view of the Eiffel Tower
services.srv-8: VIP access at Andres Carne de Res
services.srv-9: Brunch with a view of the Seine

landmark-1.name: Plaza San Martin
landmark-1.description: Heart of San Rafael. Historic square surrounded by trees, the cathedral and colonial buildings. The city's main meeting point.

landmark-2.name: San Rafael Archangel Cathedral
landmark-2.description: Main cathedral of San Rafael, neoclassical style. Located in front of Plaza San Martin.

landmark-3.name: Parque Mariano Moreno
landmark-3.description: Large green space in San Rafael with an artificial lake, trails and recreational areas. Perfect for walks and picnics.

landmark-4.name: Natural History Museum
landmark-4.description: Museum with collections of paleontology, mineralogy and regional fauna of southern Mendoza.

landmark-5.name: Plaza Francia
landmark-5.description: Iconic square in San Rafael with a central fountain, benches under old trees and a quiet atmosphere. A classic meeting point.

landmark-6.name: Parque Central
landmark-6.description: San Rafael's largest park. Green spaces, playgrounds, a lake and tree-lined trails. Perfect for families and sports.

landmark-7.name: Parque de los Ninos
landmark-7.description: Recreational space for families with children's rides, green areas and bicycle circuits. A favorite among locals.

rev-1.comment: Incredible experience! Carlos knows every corner of the Historic Center. The tacos were the best part.

rev-2.comment: The photos turned out spectacular. Maria has an impressive artistic eye.

rev-3.comment: The table had a perfect view of the Eiffel Tower. Pierre is a genius at getting reservations.

rev-4.comment: Lucia is amazing! The kayak tour through Atuel Canyon was the highlight of our trip. She knows every rock formation and its history.

rev-5.comment: The horseback ride with Martin was an authentic experience. The landscapes are stunning and he knows so much about gaucho culture.

rev-6.comment: Camila's asado was spectacular. The vineyard view at sunset, the homemade empanadas... everything was perfect. We would do it again without hesitation.

rev-7.comment: Valentina has an incredible talent for photography. The photos among the vineyards turned out beautiful, a memory we will always treasure.

rev-8.comment: Rafting with Santiago was pure adrenaline! Very professional about safety and super fun. The views of the canyon from the river are unique.

rev-9.comment: The cooking class with Isabella was magical. We learned to make Mendoza-style empanadas and then the stargazing tour... the San Rafael sky is unlike anything else.
